STARMAP Pipeline Overview
High-Level Input / Output Specification

STARMAP is a multi-stage pipeline for mapping protein structural variation
to transcriptional regulatory network (TRN) activity and drug response.

The full workflow integrates:

- 3D protein structural coordinates
- residue-level mutation annotations
- TRN gene sets
- CCLE/DepMap mutation and drug sensitivity data
- MLP-based drug sensitivity predictions
- Gi* spatial statistics
- Perturb-seq/X-Atlas validation
- Tahoe drug perturbation validation

The pipeline produces residue-level structural flatmaps, spatial TRN scores,
drug-response models, cluster-level drug enrichment scores, and experimental
validation confidence files.


============================================================
OVERALL PIPELINE ORDER
============================================================

1. NMF Structural Flatmap Generation
2. Gi* TRN Spatial Mapping
3. Drug ML Feature Generation and MLP Training
4. Drug Log-Odds Enrichment Analysis
5. Perturb-seq / X-Atlas Experimental Validation
6. Tahoe Drug Perturbation Validation


============================================================
1. NMF STRUCTURAL FLATMAP GENERATION
============================================================

Purpose:
    Converts 3D protein residue coordinates into a 2D structural flatmap.

Main input:
    ../data/3Dcoord_allgenes.csv

Required information:
    - gene name
    - residue identifier
    - x/y/z structural coordinates

Main output:
    ../output/<FIRST_LETTER>/<GENE>_nmfinfo_final.csv

Output description:
    One row per residue with:
        - 2D flatmap coordinates
        - altitude
        - NMF/KMeans cluster assignment

Used by:
    - Gi* TRN mapping
    - drug log-odds cluster mapping
    - downstream visualization


============================================================
2. Gi* TRN SPATIAL MAPPING
============================================================

Purpose:
    Maps TRN/GSEA activity onto protein structural flatmaps and identifies
    spatially localized TRN signal using Getis-Ord Gi* statistics.

Main inputs:
    - NMF flatmap output from Step 1
    - ../data/ccle_gene_position_cellline.csv
    - ../data/GSEA_files/*_GSEA.csv

Main outputs:
    ../output/3D_files/<FIRST_LETTER>/<GENE>_<TRN>_gdf.csv.gz
    ../output/scores/<FIRST_LETTER>/<GENE>_scores_all_trns.csv

Output description:
    - Residue-level spatial Gi* scores
    - Cluster-level TRN scores
    - Counts of residues with positive Gi* signal

Used by:
    - Perturb-seq validation ranking
    - pathway interpretation
    - structural TRN visualization


============================================================
3. DRUG ML FEATURE GENERATION AND MLP TRAINING
============================================================

Purpose:
    Builds mutation-level structural features, labels mutation-drug pairs
    by drug sensitivity, and trains per-drug MLP classifiers.

This stage has three substeps:

A. Distance feature generation
B. Drug sensitivity labeling
C. MLP model training

------------------------------------------------------------
3A. Distance Feature Generation
------------------------------------------------------------

Main inputs:
    - UniProt ID
    - ../data/ParsedMutations_Plus_Sites_Filtered.csv
    - AlphaFold PDB structure, downloaded automatically if needed

Main output:
    ../output/dist_files/<UNIPROT_ID>/<UNIPROT_ID>_Distances.csv

Output description:
    Mutation-level structural features including:
        - mutation C-alpha coordinates
        - distances to active sites
        - distances to binding sites
        - binding-site range centroid distances

------------------------------------------------------------
3B. Drug Sensitivity Labeling
------------------------------------------------------------

Main inputs:
    - distance feature file from Step 3A
    - ../data/CCLE_cell_line_drugsensitivity_10072023.csv
    - ../data/gene_uniprot_res_clust.csv

Main outputs:
    ../output/dist_files/<UNIPROT_ID>/<UNIPROT_ID>_Distances_Labeled.csv
    ../output/dist_files/<UNIPROT_ID>/<UNIPROT_ID>_Distances_Labeled.parquet

Output description:
    Adds:
        - drug
        - AUC_CTRP
        - binary sensitivity label
        - structural cluster assignment

------------------------------------------------------------
3C. MLP Model Training
------------------------------------------------------------

Main input:
    Labeled distance file from Step 3B

Main output:
    ../output/MLP_outputs/<UNIPROT_ID>/<DRUG>/

Output files per drug:
    - mlp_predictions.csv
    - mlp_model.joblib
    - mlp_report.txt

Output description:
    Per-drug model outputs containing:
        - predicted sensitivity labels
        - predicted sensitivity scores
        - observed labels
        - holdout and cross-validation predictions
        - model performance metrics

Used by:
    - drug log-odds enrichment analysis


============================================================
4. DRUG LOG-ODDS ENRICHMENT ANALYSIS
============================================================

Purpose:
    Converts MLP predictions into cluster-level drug enrichment scores.

Main inputs:
    - MLP predictions from Step 3C
    - NMF cluster assignments from Step 1
    - ../data/uniprot_gene_map.csv

Main outputs:
    ../output/logodds_results/<GENE>/<GENE>_<DRUG>_logodds.csv
    ../output/logodds_results/<GENE>/sorted_mlp.csv

Output description:
    For each drug and structural cluster, computes Fisher exact test
    enrichment and reports log2 odds ratios.

High positive log2 odds:
    The cluster is enriched for predicted drug-sensitive mutations.

Used by:
    - drug-response interpretation
    - flatmap visualization
    - Tahoe validation


============================================================
5. PERTURB-SEQ / X-ATLAS VALIDATION
============================================================

Purpose:
    Uses Perturb-seq gene perturbation data to test whether STARMAP-ranked
    TRNs are experimentally supported.

This stage has four substeps:

A. Download X-Atlas files
B. Compute per-cell TRN gene-set scores
C. Compare perturbed vs control cells
D. Build STARMAP-to-Perturb-seq confidence ranking

------------------------------------------------------------
5A. Download X-Atlas Files
------------------------------------------------------------

Main input:
    Figshare article ID for X-Atlas data

Main output:
    ../data/downloads/

Expected downstream file:
    ../data/downloads/HCT116_filtered_dual_guide_cells.h5ad

------------------------------------------------------------
5B. Compute Gene-Set Means
------------------------------------------------------------

Main inputs:
    - HCT116 Perturb-seq h5ad file
    - TRN gene-set CSV files

Main output:
    ../output/geneset_means.parquet

Output description:
    Per-cell mean expression scores for each TRN gene set.

------------------------------------------------------------
5C. Perturbed vs Control Statistics
------------------------------------------------------------

Main inputs:
    - selected gene
    - ../output/geneset_means.parquet
    - HCT116 Perturb-seq metadata

Main output:
    ../output/<GENE>_perturbed_df.csv

Output description:
    For each pathway, reports:
        - perturbation-control delta
        - p-value
        - number of perturbed cells
        - number of control cells

------------------------------------------------------------
5D. Perturb-seq Confidence Ranking
------------------------------------------------------------

Main inputs:
    - Perturb-seq statistics from Step 5C
    - Gi* TRN scores from Step 2

Main output:
    ../output/<GENE>_sub.csv

Output description:
    Ranked confidence table showing whether significant Perturb-seq
    pathways appear near the top of the STARMAP Gi* ranking.


============================================================
6. TAHOE DRUG PERTURBATION VALIDATION
============================================================

Purpose:
    Uses Tahoe drug perturbation pseudobulk expression data to test whether
    STARMAP-ranked drugs show experimental TRN response evidence.

This stage has two substeps:

A. Compute Tahoe ssGSEA scores for mutant vs other cell lines
B. Build empirical confidence from Tahoe drug response evidence

------------------------------------------------------------
6A. Tahoe ssGSEA Mutant vs Other Cell Lines
------------------------------------------------------------

Main inputs:
    - selected gene
    - Tahoe pseudobulk drug expression files
    - OmicsSomaticMutations.csv
    - sample_info.csv
    - TRN gene-set CSV files

Main output:
    ../output/<GENE>_mut_v_other_perdrug/

Output files per drug:
    - ssgsea_cluster_all_trns_<DRUG>.csv
    - ssgsea_other_all_trns_<DRUG>.csv

Output description:
    Drug-specific TRN activity profiles for:
        - gene-mutant cell lines
        - other cell lines

------------------------------------------------------------
6B. Tahoe Empirical Confidence
------------------------------------------------------------

Main inputs:
    - Tahoe ssGSEA outputs from Step 6A
    - STARMAP sorted drug log-odds rankings from Step 4

Main output:
    ../output/emp_files/<GENE>_emp.csv

Output description:
    Empirical confidence curve showing whether highly ranked STARMAP drugs
    are supported by Tahoe drug perturbation TRN response evidence.


============================================================
MAIN DATA DEPENDENCIES
============================================================

Core structural inputs:
    ../data/3Dcoord_allgenes.csv
    AlphaFold PDB structures

Core mutation/drug inputs:
    ../data/ParsedMutations_Plus_Sites_Filtered.csv
    ../data/CCLE_cell_line_drugsensitivity_10072023.csv
    ../data/OmicsSomaticMutations.csv
    ../data/sample_info.csv

Core mapping inputs:
    ../data/gene_uniprot_res_clust.csv
    ../data/uniprot_gene_map.csv
    ../data/ccle_gene_position_cellline.csv

Core TRN inputs:
    ../data/GSEA_files/
    ../../5_perturbseq_xatlas/data/trn_gene_sets/

Experimental validation inputs:
    ../data/downloads/HCT116_filtered_dual_guide_cells.h5ad
    ../data/tahoe_data_pseudobulk_cellline/


============================================================
FINAL PIPELINE OUTPUTS
============================================================

Structural flatmaps:
    ../output/<FIRST_LETTER>/<GENE>_nmfinfo_final.csv

TRN spatial scores:
    ../output/scores/<FIRST_LETTER>/<GENE>_scores_all_trns.csv

MLP drug sensitivity outputs:
    ../output/MLP_outputs/<UNIPROT_ID>/<DRUG>/

Drug log-odds results:
    ../output/logodds_results/<GENE>/

Perturb-seq validation:
    ../output/<GENE>_sub.csv

Tahoe validation:
    ../output/emp_files/<GENE>_emp.csv


============================================================
CONCEPTUAL SUMMARY
============================================================

STARMAP begins by flattening protein 3D structure into a residue-level 2D
map. It then projects mutation-linked TRN activity onto this map and uses
Gi* statistics to identify spatially localized pathway signals.

In parallel, structural mutation features are used to train drug-specific
MLP models that predict drug sensitivity. These predictions are collapsed
into cluster-level log-odds scores, identifying protein regions associated
with drug response.

Finally, STARMAP validates predicted TRN and drug associations using two
orthogonal experimental resources:

1. Perturb-seq / X-Atlas:
    Tests whether STARMAP-ranked TRNs are altered when the gene is perturbed.

2. Tahoe drug perturbation:
    Tests whether STARMAP-ranked drugs show corresponding TRN response
    evidence in gene-mutant cell lines.

Together, these outputs support interpretation of how mutation location
within protein structure relates to transcriptional network disruption
and drug sensitivity.
